intro:
About 396 stations are within a 30-minute commute of Meguro, expanding to 718 stations within 45 minutes. The average rent benchmark for the 45-minute commute zone is around ¥95,700. As a hub served by four lines — the JR Yamanote Line, Tokyu Meguro Line, Tokyo Metro Namboku Line, and Toei Mita Line — Meguro is easy to reach both from the Shinagawa/Kanagawa side and from northern central Tokyo and Saitama.

Meguro sits on the southwestern arc of the Yamanote Line and has developed as the western edge of the business corridor that runs through Shinagawa, Osaki, and Gotanda. Corporate headquarters in manufacturing, printing, publishing, and consulting — including the Fujifilm head office — are scattered around the area, and redevelopment near the station has added a growing number of mid-size office buildings. Corporate and professional-services roles make up a large share of jobs here, and commuters tend to skew toward a more settled age range.

The commute zone is especially well covered in the north-south direction. The Tokyu Meguro Line reaches Ookayama, Denenchofu, and Musashikosugi without a transfer; the Tokyo Metro Namboku Line covers Shirokanedai, Azabu-juban, and Komagome; and the Toei Mita Line runs direct to Sugamo and Itabashi. The Yamanote Line puts both Ebisu/Shibuya and Shinagawa within 10 minutes, and a transfer to the Yokosuka Line connects smoothly to areas south of Musashikosugi.

If you want to keep rent down, realistic candidates include Nishi-Koyama and Senzoku on the Tokyu Meguro Line, Yutenji and Gakugei-daigaku on the Tokyu Toyoko Line (transfer at Nakameguro), Nishidai and Hasune on the Toei Mita Line, and Shinmaruko and Motosumiyoshi heading south on the Tokyu Meguro Line. For those prioritizing a short commute, Togoshi and Oimachi in Shinagawa Ward and the Unoki and Yaguchinowatashi areas on the Tokyu Tamagawa Line are also popular.

| Area | Rent benchmark (single) | To Meguro | Main lines |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Musashikoyama | ¥80,000–100,000 | approx. 5–10 min | Tokyu Meguro Line |
| Ookayama | ¥80,000–100,000 | approx. 10–15 min | Tokyu Meguro Line, Tokyu Oimachi Line |
| Musashikosugi | ¥80,000–100,000 range | approx. 15–20 min | Tokyu Meguro Line, Tokyu Toyoko Line, JR Nambu Line |
| Hasune | ¥70,000–90,000 | approx. 25–30 min | Toei Mita Line |
| Denenchofu | ¥90,000–110,000 | approx. 10–15 min | Tokyu Meguro Line, Tokyu Toyoko Line |

With Kayoha, setting Meguro as your commute destination lets you compare 1831 stations across the Tokyo metropolitan area on a color-coded map in 5-minute increments, then narrow down candidate areas with an AI recommendation that surfaces 20 stations at once, alongside rent benchmarks and neighborhood profiles.

faq.q: Which areas offer affordable rent within a 30-minute commute of Meguro?
faq.a: Good starting points are Nishi-Koyama and Senzoku on the Tokyu Meguro Line, Yutenji and Gakugei-daigaku on the Tokyu Toyoko Line, Nishidai and Hasune on the Toei Mita Line, and Shinmaruko and Motosumiyoshi heading south on the Tokyu Meguro Line. Listings for singles mostly fall in the ¥70,000–100,000 range, and flexing on walking distance or building age opens up even more options.

faq.q: Which lines offer the shortest commute to Meguro?
faq.a: Four lines form the core of the 10–25 minute zone: the Yamanote Line (toward Ebisu and Gotanda), the Tokyu Meguro Line (toward Musashikoyama and Ookayama), the Tokyo Metro Namboku Line (toward Shirokanedai and Azabu-juban), and the Toei Mita Line (toward Sugamo and Itabashi). Broad north-south coverage without transfers is the defining feature of Meguro's commute zone.

faq.q: What is the Meguro area like? Is it a good place to live?
faq.a: The area around Meguro Station blends post-redevelopment offices with residential neighborhoods on the Shirokane, Kamiosaki, and Nakameguro sides. It is favored by people seeking a calm living environment, with mid-size residential districts spreading along the Meguro River and toward Gonnosukezaka. It is especially popular with singles and dual-income couples without children.

faq.q: Which areas are popular with families commuting to Meguro?
faq.a: Ookayama, Okusawa, and Denenchofu on the Tokyu Meguro Line, Toritsudaigaku and Jiyugaoka on the Tokyu Toyoko Line, and the Musashikosugi area on the Nambu and Tokyu Meguro Lines tend to offer parks, good schools, and supermarkets, making them popular with families. Commute times mostly stay within 10–25 minutes.

faq.q: Are there stations where you can get a seat or catch a first-departure train when commuting to Meguro?
faq.a: The Tokyu Meguro Line has trains originating at Hiyoshi, giving commuters from the Musashikosugi and Shinmaruko side a chance to ride seated. On the Toei Mita Line, the Nishi-Takashimadaira and Takashimadaira direction works well, and on the JR Yamanote Line, choosing early-morning services via Osaki and Shinagawa improves your odds of getting a seat.
